body:
DESIGN-STAGE (Myk's merge, P6 — it widens what a renderer IS). ORIGIN (Myk, 2026-07-24, goodnight message): support renderers rendered EITHER by the React router/app Loam ships OR by Claude Interactive Artifacts, with support for pass-through MCPs. This is the deployment half of the demo vision ('ingest these deltas, run your own artifact, provisioned to your account, serve my app that way' — the $10M conversation): a Claude artifact is a valid, zero-infra deployment surface for a Loam app.

THE SHAPE TO DESIGN (recommendations to carry into the working spec):
(1) A renderer binding gains a TARGET declaration — 'host' (today's worker-rendered HTML, the default; nothing changes for existing bindings) vs 'artifact' (a self-contained page intended to run inside Claude's artifact sandbox). Shape-distinguishable vocabulary per the §20 corollary; absent target reads as 'host', so no migration.
(2) The DATA PATH is the door, not the bundle: an artifact cannot import the store, so it speaks to a Loam server over the network — and the artifact sandbox's CSP blocks direct fetch, which is exactly what pass-through MCP solves: the viewer's claude.ai MCP connector calls the store's MCP door (already served at /:mount/mcp) with the VIEWER'S credentials. So 'provisioned to your account' is literal: each viewer's artifact talks to their own store (or a shared one) through their own connector — identity rides the connector, never the bundle.
(3) WHAT LOAM EMITS for an artifact target: a build/pack door that renders the artifact page — bundle + the mount/schema coordinates it should speak to — as a single self-contained HTML the operator (or Claude) publishes as the artifact. The renderer bundle stays deltas; the artifact page is an EMISSION from them (content-addressed like any §23.10 build product), so the §23.6 law ('an app never outlives its source') keeps holding: withdraw the binding and the pack door stops emitting.
(4) CAPABILITY HONESTY: an artifact-target renderer runs OUTSIDE §23.9's worker confinement — its confinement is Claude's sandbox, and its authority is the viewer's MCP grant. The working spec must state this trust swap plainly: host-target = operator's sandbox + operator's doors; artifact-target = Anthropic's sandbox + viewer's credentials. Neither is weaker; they are different trust roots, and the binding's target declaration is what makes the difference legible.
(5) FEDERATION FIT: the artifact story composes with T77's bridges and §28 — 'interacting through Claude' is interacting via federation channels (Myk, 2026-07-23): the artifact's writes land wherever the viewer's MCP door points, subject to that store's admission.

DESIGN QUESTIONS for P1: does the pack door live on the gateway (a §23 sibling of serveRoute) or in the CLI (a build step)? Recommendation: gateway door — it must respect withdrawal live. Does an artifact-target binding still declare `consumes` (yes — the pack embeds the field coordinates so the artifact queries only what the binding promised)? What is the smallest MCP tool-surface an artifact needs (query + mutate + subscribe-poll)? — enumerate against the existing /:mount/mcp handler rather than inventing.

Evidence: the 2026-07-23 investor-demo conversation (claude artifacts as deployment surface, window.claude.mcp capability); the 2026-07-24 goodnight message; src/server/http.ts handleMcp (the door that already exists).

edges.contract: An artifact-targeted renderer talks to the store over the network door; a module's app must be reachable at its own mount for the artifact to address it, so dynamic mounts land first.

title: Renderer dual-target: Loam's shipped React host OR a Claude Interactive Artifact, with pass-through MCP
